1: Vision. From chazah; a sight, i.e. A dream, revelation, or oracle.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
From yadah; celebrated; Jehudah, the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory.
Probably foundation of peace, capital city of all Isr.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
Or Yshayahuw; from yasha' and Yahh; Jah has saved; Jeshajah, the name of seven Israelites.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
Strong, the father of Isaiah. From 'amats; strong; Amots, an Israelite.
See, behold. A primitive root; to gaze at; mentally, to perceive, contemplate; specifically, to have a vision of.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Or Uzziyahuw; from oz and Yahh; strength of Jah; Uzzijah, the name of five Israelites.
The LORD is perfect, three Isr. From Yhovah and tam; Jehovah perfect; Jotham, the name of three Israelites.
He has grasped, two Isr. From 'achaz; possessor; Achaz, the name of a Jewish king and of an Israelite.
Yah has strengthened, a king of Judah, also several other Isr.
From yadah; celebrated; Jehudah, the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory.
10: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
A chief, ruler. From qatsah in the sense of determining; a magistrate or other leader.
A Canaanite city near the Dead Sea. From an unused root meaning to scorch; burnt district; Sedom, a place near the Dead Sea.
A primitive root; probably to expand; but used only as a denominative from 'ozen; to broaden out the ear, i.e. to listen.
Direction, instruction, law. Or torah; from yarah; a precept or statute, especially the Decalogue or Pentateuch.
Plural of 'elowahh; gods in the ordinary sense; but specifically used of the supreme God
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
A city in the Jordan Valley. From amar; a heap; Amorah, a place in Palestine.
11: Multitude, abundance, greatness. From rabab; abundance.
A sacrifice. From zabach; properly, a slaughter, i.e. The flesh of an animal; by implication, a sacrifice.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To be sated, satisfied or surfeited. Or sabeay; a primitive root; to sate, i.e. Fill to satisfaction.
Whole burnt offering. Or mowlah; feminine active participle of alah; a step or; usually a holocaust.
From the same as 'uwl; properly, strength; hence, anything strong; specifically a chief; also a ram; a pilaster; an oak or other strong tree.
Or cheleb; from an unused root meaning to be fat; fat, whether literally or figuratively; hence, the richest or choice part.
A fatling, fatlings. From mara' in the sense of grossness, through the idea of domineering; stall-fed; often a beeve.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; properly, to incline to; by implication to bend; figuratively, to be pleased with, desire.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
Young bull, steer. Or par; from parar; a bullock.
A lamb. From an unused root meaning to dominate; a ram.
Male goat. Or sattud; from athad; prepared, i.e. Full grown; spoken only of he-goats, or leaders of the people.
12: To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
An interrogative pronoun of persons, as mah is of things, who?; also whoever; often used in oblique construction with prefix or suffix.
To seek. A primitive root; to search out; by implication, to strive after.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
To trample. A primitive root; to tread upon.
13: To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Or shav; from the same as show' in the sense of desolating; evil, literally or morally; figuratively idolatry, uselessness.
From an unused root meaning to apportion, i.e. Bestow; a donation; euphemistically, tribute; specifically a sacrificial offering.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.
Smoke, odor of (burning) sacrifice, incense. From qatar; a fumigation.
Or tonebah; feminine active participle of ta'ab; properly, something disgusting, i.e. an abhorrence; especially idolatry or an idol.
New moon, a month. From chadash; the new moon; by implication, a month.
Sabbath. Intensive from shabath; intermission, i.e the Sabbath.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
A convocation, convoking, reading. From qara'; something called out, i.e. A public meeting; also a rehearsal.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be able, have power. Or yakowl; a primitive root; to be able, literally or morally.
From an unused root perhaps meaning properly, to pant; strictly nothingness; also trouble. Vanity, wickedness; specifically an idol.
An assembly. Or matsereth; from atsar; an assembly, especially on a festival or holiday.
14: From naphash; properly, a breathing creature, i.e. Animal of vitality; used very widely in a literal, accommodated or figurative sense.
To hate. A primitive root; to hate.
New moon, a month. From chadash; the new moon; by implication, a month.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A burden. From tarach; a burden.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To be weary or impatient. A primitive root; to tire; to be disgusted.
Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.
15: To spread out, spread. A primitive root; to break apart, disperse, etc.
Hollow or flat of the hand, palm, sole (of the foot), a pan. From kaphaph; the hollow hand or palm; figuratively, power.
To conceal. A primitive root; to veil from sight, i.e. Conceal.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To be or become much, many or great. A primitive root; to increase.
Prayer. From palal; intercession, supplication; by implication, a hymn.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To be full, to fill. Or malae; a primitive root, to fill or be full of, in a wide application.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
16: To wash, wash off or away, bathe. A primitive root; to lave.
To be clear, clean or pure. A primitive root; to be translucent; figuratively, to be innocent.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
Badness, evil. From ra'a'; badness, physically or morally.
A deed, practice. From alal; an act.
From nagad; a front, i.e. Part opposite; specifically a counterpart, or mate; usually over against or before.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.
To cease. A primitive root; properly, to be flabby, i.e. desist; be lacking or idle.
A primitive root; properly, to spoil; figuratively, to make good for nothing, i.e. Bad.
17: A primitive root; properly, to goad, i.e. to teach:(un-) accustomed, X diligently, expert, instruct, learn, skilful, teach(-er,ing).
To be good, well, glad, or pleasing. A primitive root; to be make well, literally or figuratively.
A primitive root; properly, to tread or frequent; usually to follow; by implication, to seek or ask; specifically to worship.
To go straight, go on, advance. Or rasher; a primitive root; to be straight; figuratively, to go forward, be honest, proper.
The ruthless (ones). From chamets; properly, violent; by implication, a robber.
An orphan. From an unused root meaning to be lonely; a bereaved person.
Or ruwb; a primitive root; properly, to toss, i.e. Grapple; mostly figuratively, to wrangle, i.e. Hold a controversy; to defend.
A widow. Fem of 'alman; a widow; also a desolate place.
18: To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
To decide, adjudge, prove. A primitive root; to be right; reciprocal, to argue; causatively, to decide, justify or convict.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
A sin. From chata'; a crime or its penalty.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Scarlet. Of uncertain derivation; crimson, properly, the insect or its color, also stuff dyed with it.
To be, white, to make bricks. A primitive root; to be white; also to make bricks.
If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To be red. To show blood, i.e. Flush or turn rosy.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Wool. From an unused root probably meaning to be shaggy; wool.
19: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To be willing, to consent. A primitive root; to breathe after, i.e. to be acquiescent.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Good things, goods, goodness. From towb; good, in the widest sense, especially goodness, beauty, gladness, welfare.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
2: To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Heaven, sky. Dual of an unused singular shameh; from an unused root meaning to be lofty; the sky.
A primitive root; probably to expand; but used only as a denominative from 'ozen; to broaden out the ear, i.e. to listen.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
To grow up, become great. A primitive root; properly, to twist, i.e. To be large.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
To be high or exalted, rise. A primitive root; to be high actively, to rise or raise.
They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
To rebel, transgress. A primitive root; to break away, i.e. Trespass, apostatize, quarrel.
20: If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
To refuse. A primitive root; to refuse.
To be contentious or rebellious. A primitive root; to be bitter; to rebel.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
A sword. From charab; drought; also a cutting instrument, as a knife, sword, or other sharp implement.
Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
21: How?. Also eykah; and teykakah; prolonged from 'ay; how? Or how!; also where.
Full. From male'; full or filling; also fulness; adverbially, fully.
A town, city. From qarah in the sense of flooring, i.e. Building; a city.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
To commit fornication, be a harlot. A primitive root; to commit adultery; figuratively, to commit idolatry.
She once was full of justice;
Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.
Or liyn; a primitive root; to stop; by implication, to stay permanently; hence to be obstinate.
Now. From eth; at this time, whether adverb, conjunction or expletive.
To murder, slay. A primitive root; properly, to dash in pieces, i.e. Kill, especially to murder.
22: Silver, money. From kacaph; silver; by implication, money.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
A moving back or away, dross. Or cuwg; from cuwg in the sense of refuse; scoria.
A drink, liquor. From caba'; potation, concretely, or abstractly.
To circumcise, weaken. A primitive root; properly, to cut down or reduce, i.e. By implication, to adulterate.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
23: Chieftain, chief, ruler, official, captain, prince. From sarar; a head person.
Stubborn or rebellious. A primitive root; to turn away, i.e. be refractory.
United, associate, companion. From chabar; an associate.
A thief. From ganab; a stealer.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To love. Or raheb; a primitive root; to have affection for.
A present, bribe. From shachad; a donation.
To pursue, chase, persecute. A primitive root; to run after (of time) gone by).
A reward, bribe. From shalam; a bribe.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
An orphan. From an unused root meaning to be lonely; a bereaved person.
Strife, dispute. Or rib; from riyb; a contest.
A widow. Fem of 'alman; a widow; also a desolate place.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
24: So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
Lord. Or adon; from an unused root; sovereign, i.e. Controller.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
Ah! alas! ha! A prolonged form of how; oh!
A primitive root; properly, to sigh, i.e. Breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. to pity, console or rue; or to avenge.
Narrow, a tight place, a pebble, an opponent. Or tsar; from tsarar; narrow; a tight place; also a pebble; an opponent.
To avenge, take vengeance. A primitive root; to grudge, i.e. Avenge or punish.
Hating, an adversary. Or owyeb; active participle of 'ayab; hating; an adversary.
25: A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To smelt, refine, test. A primitive root; to fuse, i.e. Refine.
The same as bor; vegetable lye; used as a soap for washing, or a flux for metals.
A moving back or away, dross. Or cuwg; from cuwg in the sense of refuse; scoria.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Alloy, tin, dross. From badal; alloy; by analogy, tin.
26: A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Former, first, chief. Or riishon; from ri'shah; first, in place, time or rank.
To advise, counsel. A primitive root; to advise; reflexively, to deliberate or resolve.
A beginning. From chalal in the sense of opening; a commencement; rel. Original.
The hind or following part. From 'achar; properly, the hind part; generally used as an adverb or conjunction, after.
So, thus. From kuwn; properly, set upright; hence just; but usually rightly or so.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
Rightness, righteousness. From tsadaq; the right; also equity or prosperity.
A town, city. From qarah in the sense of flooring, i.e. Building; a city.
27: A mountain in Jer., also a name for Jer. The same as tsiyuwn; Tsijon, a mountain of Jerusalem.
To ransom. A primitive root; to sever, i.e. Ransom; gener. To release, preserve.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
28: To rebel, transgress. A primitive root; to break away, i.e. Trespass, apostatize, quarrel.
Sinful, sinners. Intensively from chata'; a criminal, or one accounted guilty.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
Or sheber; from shabar; a fracture, figuratively, ruin; specifically, a solution.
To loosen, relinquish, permit. A primitive root; to loosen, i.e. Relinquish, permit, etc.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To be complete, at an end, finished, accomplished, or spent. A primitive root; to end, whether intransitive or transitived.
29: To be ashamed. A primitive root; properly, to pale, i.e. By implication to be ashamed; also to be disappointed or delayed.
From the same as 'uwl; properly, strength; hence, anything strong; specifically a chief; also a ram; a pilaster; an oak or other strong tree.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To desire, take pleasure in. A primitive root; to delight in.
A primitive root: to blush; figuratively, to be ashamed, disappointed; causatively, to shame, reproach.
A garden. Feminine of gan; a garden.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To choose. A primitive root; properly, to try, i.e. select.
3: A head of cattle (bullock, ox, etc.). From shuwr; a bullock.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
A primitive root; to erect, i.e. Create; by extension, to procure, especially by purchase; by implication to own.
A male ass. Or chamor; from chamar; a male ass.
Owner, lord. From ba'al; a master; hence, a husband, or owner.
A crib, feeding trough. From 'abac; a manger or stall.

Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

A terebinth. Feminine of 'ayil; an oak or other strong tree.
Leaf, leafage. From alah; a leaf; collectively, foliage.
A primitive root; to wilt; generally, to fall away, fail, faint; figuratively, to be foolish or wicked; causatively, to despise, disgrace.
A garden. Feminine of gan; a garden.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.

Tow (for making thread). From na'ar; something shaken out, i.e. Tow.
Doing, deed, work. From pa'al; an act or work.
A spark. From natsats; a spark.
Two (a card. number). Dual of sheniy; feminine shttayim; two; also twofold.

A primitive root; properly, to miss; hence to sin; by inference, to forfeit, lack, expiate, repent, lead astray, condemn.

Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).

Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.

To add. A primitive root; to add or augment.

Head. From an unused root apparently meaning to shake; the head, whether literal or figurative.

The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.

To give a ringing cry. A primitive root; properly, to creak, i.e. To shout.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.

From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.

Man, mankind. From 'adam; ruddy i.e. A human being.
Gold. From katham; properly, something carved out, i.e. Ore; hence, gold.
Or pophiyr; and fowphir; of uncertain derivation; Ophir, the name of a son of Joktan, and of a gold region in the East.

To arise, stand up, stand. A primitive root; to rise.

Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.

To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.

A chief, ruler. From qatsah in the sense of determining; a magistrate or other leader.
A primitive root; properly, to wave to and fro; figuratively, to rove, flee, or to drive away.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.

Not. From balah; properly, a failure; by implication nothing; usually not at all; also lest.
To spread out, spread. A primitive root; to break apart, disperse, etc.
From nacac; a flag; also a sail; by implication, a flagstaff; generally a signal; figuratively, a token.
At that time. A demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
Abundance. From rabah; properly, increasing; as noun, greatness, or greatly.
Booty, prey. The same as ad in the sense of the aim of an attack; booty.
A prey, spoil, plunder, booty. From shalal; booty.
To be smooth, to apportion, separate. A primitive root; to be smooth; by implication to apportion or separate.
To spoil, plunder. A primitive root; to plunder.
Spoiling, robbery, spoil, booty. From bazaz; plunder.

Inhabitant, neighbor. From shakan; a resident; by extension, a fellow-citizen.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
A primitive root; properly, to be rubbed or worn; hence to be weak, sick, afflicted; or to grieve, make sick; also to stroke, entreat.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.

Or nacah ('abad); a primitive root; to lift, in a great variety of applications, literal and figurative, absol. And rel.

A primitive root; properly, to wave to and fro; figuratively, to rove, flee, or to drive away.

Uplifting, arising. From the active participle of ramam; exaltation.

(a kind of) locust. From chacal; the ravager, i.e. A locust.

Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.

To cry, cry out, call. A primitive root; to shriek; to proclaim.

Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
To weep, bewail. A primitive root; to weep; generally to bemoan.

A way, path. From 'arach; a well-trodden road; also a caravan.
To repose, desist from exertion. A primitive root; to repose, i.e. Desist from exertion; used in many implied relations.

To break up, to violate, frustrate. A primitive root; to break up
Excitement. Or par; or ayar; from uwr a city in the widest sense.
To spurn, to disappear. A primitive root; to spurn; also to disappear.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

To mourn. A primitive root; to bewail.

To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
From arab; a desert; especially the sterile valley of the Jordan and its continuation to the Red Sea.

The same as karmel; Karmel, the name of a hill and of a town in Palestine.
To shake, shake out or off. A primitive root; to tumble about.

Rarely kabod; from kabad; properly, weight, but only figuratively in a good sense, splendor or copiousness.

To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.

To be stout, strong, bold, alert. A primitive root; to be alert, physically or mentally.

5: At that time. A demonstrative adverb; at that time or place; also as a conjunction, therefore.
An eye. Probably a primitive word; an eye; by analogy, a fountain.

To leap. A primitive root; to spring.
A hart, stag, deer. An intensive form of 'ayil; a stag or male deer.

To give a ringing cry. A primitive root; properly, to creak, i.e. To shout.
Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
To cleave, break open or through. A primitive root; to cleave; generally, to rend, break, rip or open.
A pasture, a desert, speech. From dabar in the sense of driving; a pasture; by implication, a desert; also speech.
Or nachlah; or nachalah; from nachal in its original sense; a stream, especially a winter torrent; a valley; also a shaft.
From arab; a desert; especially the sterile valley of the Jordan and its continuation to the Red Sea.

Waters, water. Dual of a primitive noun; water; figuratively, juice; by euphemism, urine, semen.
Or navah; from navah; at home; hence lovely; also a home, of God, men, flocks, or wild animals.

(place of) lying down, resting place, dwelling place. From rabats; a couch or place of repose.

A rush, reed, papyrus. From gama'; properly, an absorbent, i.e. The bulrush; specifically the papyrus.

A palace, temple. Probably from yakol; a large public building, such as a palace or temple.
From balal; confusion; Babel, including Babylonia and the Babylonian empire.
Yah has strengthened, a king of Judah, also several other Isr.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Or Yshayahuw; from yasha' and Yahh; Jah has saved; Jeshajah, the name of seven Israelites.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
Firmness, faithfulness, truth. Contracted from 'aman; stability; certainty, truth, trustworthiness.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.

1: Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Or shibrah; from shaba'; a primitive cardinal number; seven; also seven times; by implication, a week; by extension, an indefinite number.
Woman, wife, female. Feminine of 'iysh or 'enowsh; irregular plural, nashiym; a woman.
A primitive root; to fasten upon; hence, to seize, be strong, obstinate; to bind, restrain, conquer.
One. A numeral from 'achad; properly, united, i.e. One; or first.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Bread, food. From lacham; food, especially bread, or grain.
Or labesh; a primitive root; properly, wrap around, i.e. to put on a garment or clothe, literally or figuratively.
A wrapper, mantle. Perhaps by permutation for the feminine of cemel; a dress, especially a mantle.
The same as raq as a noun; properly, leanness, i.e. limitation; only adverbial, merely, or conjunctional, although.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
To gather, remove. A primitive root; to gather for any purpose; hence, to receive, take away, i.e. Remove.

A sprout, growth. From tsamach; a sprout, literal or figurative.

To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Splendor, a gazelle. From tsabah in the sense of prominence; splendor; also a gazelle.

Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Arrogance, majesty, ornament. From ga'ah; the same as ga'avah.
Beauty, glory. Or tiphereth; from pa'ar; ornament.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
An escape. Or pletah; feminine of paliyt; deliverance; concretely, an escaped portion.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.

Probably foundation of peace, capital city of all Isr.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Sacred, holy. Or qadosh; from qadash; sacred; God, an angel, a saint, a sanctuary.

If. A primitive particle; used very widely as demonstrative, lo!
Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.

Breath, wind, spirit. From ruwach; wind; by resemblance breath.

To create, to cut down, select, feed. A primitive root; to create; to cut down, select, feed.

The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.

Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.

1: A primitive root; properly, to sigh, i.e. Breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. to pity, console or rue; or to avenge.
A primitive root; properly, to sigh, i.e. Breathe strongly; by implication, to be sorry, i.e. to pity, console or rue; or to avenge.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.

10: Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
To come in, come, go in, go. A primitive root; to go or come.
Strong, stout, mighty. From chazaq; strong.

To rule, have dominion, reign. A primitive root; to rule.
Lo! behold! Prolongation for hen; lo!
Hire, wages. From sakar; payment of contract; concretely, salary, fare, maintenance; by implication, compensation, benefit.

A work, recompense. Feminine passive participle of pa'al; work.

Arm, shoulder, strength. Or zroao; and zrowpah; or zrowah; from zara'; the arm, or the foreleg; figuratively, force.

in the hollow of his hand,
Hollow hand, handful. From an unused root meaning to hollow out; the palm; by extension, a handful.

A span. From zarah; the spread of the fingers, i.e. A span.

Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.

Or ruwb; a primitive root; properly, to toss, i.e. Grapple; mostly figuratively, to wrangle, i.e. Hold a controversy; to defend.

Mouth. From pa'ah; the mouth, whether literal or figurative; specifically edge, portion or side; adverbially according to.
To speak. A primitive root; perhaps properly, to arrange; but used figuratively, to speak; rarely to subdue.
Senselessness, disgrace. Feminine of nabal; foolishness, i.e. wickedness; concretely, a crime; by extension, punishment.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
A primitive root; to turn back transitively or intransitively, literally or figuratively; generally to retreat; often adverbial, again.
Or rod; from uwd; properly, iteration or continuance; used only adverbially, again, repeatedly, still, more.
A primitive root; to stretch or spread out; by implication, to bend away; used in a great variety of application.
18: To kindle, consume, to be, brutish. A primitive root; to kindle, i.e. Consume; also to be(-come) brutish.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Thornbushes. From shiyth; scrub or trash, i.e. Wild growth of weeds or briers.
A thorn, adamant, flint. From shamar in the original sense of pricking; a thorn; also a gem, probably the diamond.
To kindle, burn. A primitive root; to burn or set on fire; figuratively, to desolate.
From an unused root probably meaning to thicken with verdure; a copse of bushes; hence, a forest; hence, honey in the comb.
A thicket. From cabak, a copse.
To turn. A primitive root; probably to coil upward.
Majesty. From ga'ah; the same as ga'avah.
Smoke. From ashan; smoke, literally or figuratively.
19: Overflow, arrogance, fury. Feminine of eber; an outburst of passion.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Perhaps to burn. A primitive root; probably to glow, i.e. be desolated.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Fuel. From 'akal; something eaten, i.e. Fuel.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To spare. A primitive root; to commiserate; by implication, to spare.
A brother. A primitive word; a brother (like 'ab).
2: From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
From chashak; the dark; hence darkness; figuratively, misery, destruction, death, ignorance, sorrow, wickedness.
To see. A primitive root; to see, literally or figuratively.
Great. Or gadol; from gadal; great; hence, older; also insolent.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Earth, land. From an unused root probably meaning to be firm; the earth.
Death-like shadow, deep shadow. From tsel and maveth; shade of death, i.e. The grave.
A light. From 'owr; illumination or luminary.
To shine. A primitive root; to glitter; causatively, to illuminate.
20: To cut, divide. A primitive root; to cut down or off; to destroy, divide, exclude, or decide.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Right hand. From yaman; the right hand or side of a person or other object; locally, the south.
To be hungry. A primitive root; to hunger.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To be sated, satisfied or surfeited. Or sabeay; a primitive root; to sate, i.e. Fill to satisfaction.
Man. Contracted for 'enowsh; a man as an individual or a male person; often used as an adjunct to a more definite term.
To eat. A primitive root; to eat.
Flesh. From basar; flesh; by extension, body, person; also The pudenda of a man.
Arm, shoulder, strength. Or zroao; and zrowpah; or zrowah; from zara'; the arm, or the foreleg; figuratively, force.
21: From nashah; causing to forget; Menashsheh, a grandson of Jacob, also the tribe descended from him, and its territory.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Dual of masculine form of 'Ephraath; double fruit; Ephrajim, a son of Joseph; also the tribe descended from him, and its territory.

Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
From nashah; causing to forget; Menashsheh, a grandson of Jacob, also the tribe descended from him, and its territory.
Unitedness. From yachad; properly, a unit, i.e. unitedly.
They. Or hemmah; masculine plural from halak; they.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
From yadah; celebrated; Jehudah, the name of five Israelites; also of the tribe descended from the first, and of its territory.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
A nostril, nose, face, anger. From 'anaph; properly, the nose or nostril; hence, the face, and occasionally a person; also ire.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.

3: To be or become much, many or great. A primitive root; to increase.
Rarely goy; apparently from the same root as gevah; a foreign nation; hence, a Gentile; also a troop of animals, or a flight of locusts.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To grow up, become great. A primitive root; properly, to twist, i.e. To be large.
Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.
Rejoice, be glad. A primitive root; probably to brighten up, i.e. be blithe or gleesome.
Face, faces. Plural of an unused noun; the face; used in a great variety of applications; also as a preposition.
Joy, gladness, mirth. From sameach; blithesomeness or glee.
From qatsar; severed, i.e. Harvest, the crop, the time, the reaper, or figuratively; also a limb.
Who, which, that. A primitive relative pronoun; who, which, what, that; also when, where, how, because, in order that, etc.
To rejoice. Or guwl; a primitive root; properly, to spin round, i.e. Usually rejoice, or fear.
To be smooth, to apportion, separate. A primitive root; to be smooth; by implication to apportion or separate.
A prey, spoil, plunder, booty. From shalal; booty.
4: Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
The same as midyan; Midjan, a son of Abraham; also his country and his descendants.
A primitive root; properly, to prostrate; hence, to break down, either by violence, or by confusion and fear.
A yoke. Or mowl; from alal; a yoke, literally or figuratively.
A burden. (only in the form cubbal; from cabal; a load.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
Or mattah; from natah; a branch; figuratively, a tribe; also a rod, whether for chastising, ruling, throwing, or walking.
Shoulder. From shakam; the neck as the place of burdens; figuratively, the spur of a hill.
Rod, staff, club, scepter, tribe. From an unused root probably meaning to branch off; a scion, i.e. a stick Or a clan.
To press, drive, oppress, exact. A primitive root; to drive; by implication, to tax, harass, tyrannize.
5: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
To tread, tramp. A primitive root; to be miry; used only as denominative from c'own; to shoe, i.e. a soldier shod.
Sandal, boot (of a soldier). From ca'an; perhaps a military boot
A quaking, shaking. From ra'ash; vibration, bounding, uproar.
A wrapper, mantle. Perhaps by permutation for the feminine of cemel; a dress, especially a mantle.
To roll, roll away. A primitive root; to roll.
Blood. From damam; blood of man or an animal; by analogy, the juice of the grape; figuratively bloodshed.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Fuel. From 'akal; something eaten, i.e. Fuel.
A fire. A primitive word; fire.
6: Child, son, boy, youth. From yalad; something born, i.e. A lad or offspring.
A primitive root; to bear young; causatively, to beget; medically, to act as midwife; specifically, to show lineage.
Son. From banah; a son, in the widest sense (like 'ab, 'ach, etc.).
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
To fall out, come to pass, become, be. A primitive root; to exist, i.e. Be or become, come to pass.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Shoulder. From shakam; the neck as the place of burdens; figuratively, the spur of a hill.
To call, proclaim, read. A primitive root; to call out to.
A primitive word; an appellation, as a mark or memorial of individuality; by implication honor, authority, character.
A wonder. From pala'; a miracle.
To advise, counsel. A primitive root; to advise; reflexively, to deliberate or resolve.
Strong, mighty. Or gibbor; intensive from the same as geber; powerful; by implication, warrior, tyrant.
God, in pl. gods. Shortened from 'ayil; strength; as adjective, mighty; especially the Almighty.
Father. A primitive word; father, in a literal and immediate, or figurative and remote application).
Chieftain, chief, ruler, official, captain, prince. From sarar; a head person.
Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
7: Abundance. From rabah; properly, increasing; as noun, greatness, or greatly.
Or shalom; from shalam; safe, i.e. well, happy, friendly; also welfare, i.e. Health, prosperity, peace.
As if from a primitive root meaning to be nothing or not exist; a non-entity; generally used as a negative particle.
End. Contracted from qatsats; an extremity; adverbially after.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Seat of honor, throne. Or kicceh; from kacah; properly, covered, i.e. A throne.
Perhaps beloved one, a son of Jesse. Rarely; Daviyd; from the same as dowd; loving; David, the youngest son of Jesse.
Properly, the same as al used as a preposition; above, over, upon, or against in a great variety of applications.
Kingdom, sovereignty, dominion, reign. From malak; dominion, i.e. the estate or the country.
A primitive root; properly, to be erect; hence to set up, in a great variety of applications, whether literal, or figurative.
Apparent contracted from 'owth in the demonstrative sense of entity; properly, self.
To support, sustain, stay. A primitive root; to suport.
Righteousness. From tsadaq; rightness, subjectively, objectively, morally or figuratively.
Now. From eth; at this time, whether adverb, conjunction or expletive.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Ardor, zeal, jealousy. From qana'; jealousy or envy.
The proper name of the God of Israel. From hayah; self-Existent or Eternal; Jehovah, Jewish national name of God.
Or tsbadah; from tsaba'; a mass of persons, especially reg. Organized for war; by implication, a campaign, literally or figuratively.
To do, make. A primitive root; to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application.
Hereby in it, likewise, the one other, same, she, so much, such deed, that. Irregular feminine of zeh; this.
8: Lord. Am emphatic form of 'adown; the Lord.
To send. A primitive root; to send away, for, or out.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
A son of Isaac, also his desc. From aqab; heel-catcher; Jaakob, the Israelitish patriarch.
To fall, lie. A primitive root; to fall, in a great variety of applications.
From sarah and 'el; he will rule as God; Jisrael, a symbolical name of Jacob; also of his posterity.
9: The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
A primitive root; to know; used in a great variety of senses, figuratively, literally, euphemistically and inferentially.
Dual of masculine form of 'Ephraath; double fruit; Ephrajim, a son of Joseph; also the tribe descended from him, and its territory.
A primitive root; properly, to sit down; by implication, to dwell, to remain; causatively, to settle, to marry.
Capital of N. kingdom of Isr. From the active participle of shamar; watch-station; Shomeron, a place in Palestine.
Majesty, pride. From ga'ah; arrogance or majesty; by implication, ornament.
Inner man, mind, will, heart. From labab; the heart; used also like leb.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
